A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO, WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 105 MPH, DAMAGED OR DESTROYED MORE THAN 10 OUTBUILDINGS AND OVERTURNED TWO RVS. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A FEW HOMES. A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED WITH LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN OFF OF NUMEROUS TREES. THE TORNADO PATH WAS 0.7 MILES LONG WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO CROSSED I-49 WITH VISIBLE DEBRIS IN THE MEDIAN.
TREE DOWN ON A HOME.
AVILLA FIRE DEPT IS REPORTING PIECES OF A BARN (DEBRIS) IS ON A FENCE AND POWER LINES DOWN JUST TO THE EAST OF COUNTY ROAD 50 AND NUTMEG IN JASPER COUNTY.

AN NWS SURVEY REVEALED THAT STRAIGHT-LINE WIND GUSTS CAUSED ROOF DAMAGE TO A TURKEY FARM. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO, WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 77 MPH, HEAVILY DAMAGED A MACHINE BARN. TIN WAS BLOWN IN A CYCLONIC MANNER. THE TORNADO ALSO BROKE SEVERAL TREE LIMBS. THE TORNADO PATH WAS 1.6 MILES LONG. WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 200 YARDS.

*** 5 INJ *** INTERMIT TORNADO PATH WITH MAINLY EF1 DAMAGE AND SOME LOW END EF2 DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM WEST OF VICHY TO THE NORTHEAST OF VICHY. MULTIPLE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH. A HOUSE HAD SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO EXTERIOR WALLS AND SOME INTERIOR WALLS. NEARLY TOTAL LOSS OF ROOF ON TWO METAL FRAME BUILDINGS. ANOTHER METAL BUILDING HAD ITS WEST WALLS BLOW INWARD AND EAST WALLS OUTWARD. FIVE MINOR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

LARGE BRANCH OF A TREE CAME DOWN AND FELL A NEIGHBORING YARD ON BURFORD STREET. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

AN INTERMITTENT EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR FAIR GROVE, MISSOURI AND TRACKED TO THE NORTHWEST. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE 77 MPH WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. AT THE START POINT OF THE TORNADO, A HOME SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE TO SIDING. AS THE TORNADO MOVED TO THE NORTHWEST, SEVERAL LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH. THE TORNADO PATH CROSSED HIGHWAY 65 JUST NORTH OF FAIR GROVE, AT WHICH TWO FARM OUTBUILDINGS WERE IMPACTED. THIS INCLUDED THE COMPLETE COLLAPSE OF A FARM OUTBUILDING AND DAMAGE TO ANOTHER OUTBUILDING. ROOF SHEET METAL FROM ONE OUTBUILDING WAS TOSSED AROUND 200 YARDS. THERE WAS THE PRESENCE OF A TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNATURE (TDS) ALONG THE TORNADO PATH. THE TORNADO LIFTED ALONG A WOOD LINE JUST NORTHWEST OF THE FARM OUTBUILDINGS.
65 AND WOODSTOCK ROAD UNPASSABLE.
